title: READABILITY: Explain atomic swap sentinel logic in keepalive timeout check
description: relay/src/ws.rs:269 — The line `if \!pong_write.swap(false, Ordering::Relaxed)` uses an atomic swap as a sentinel for keepalive timeout detection. The comment 'If the previous Ping was not acked by now' doesn't explain *how* the swap detects this: on each ping_interval tick, swap(false) returns the previous state. If it returns true, pong was received since the last tick; if false, it wasn't. A reader unfamiliar with atomic sentinel patterns won't understand the logic at first glance. Fix: expand the comment to explain the swap mechanics and why Ordering::Relaxed is safe here (single-threaded access on this flag across just ping_interval and pong receive paths).
close_reason: P4 readability/comment items. Not blocking correctness. Deferred — file separately if prioritized.
